content.body: I used ShipMonk as my 3PL for a silver jewelry company for almost 2 years... To start with, the onboarding was really frustrating and took WAY longer than the expectation that was set. Then we spent weeks changing the processes because their fulfillment team could not comprehend the proper way to package our simple items in their boxes correctly, despite documents and videos being provided. We stuck with it because we figured it was an "investment" that would pay off once we got it right. So we did stick with it, until I had virtually no other choice than to cancel them and get my inventory back due to the amount of my items that went "missing" while in their possession. My customers would regularly receive empty boxes, even though our in-house team packaged them and sealed them. This caused a lot of issues and multiple poor reviews. AND, when we finally received all of our remaining inventory back, not only was everything extremely dusty, but DOZENS of items were missing from the expected line sheet that they provided. Unfortunately, I think their staff is ruining what could be a great service, because the platform was excellent. But they have crooked staff in the fulfillment department and some lazy/uninterested staff in their customer service department. If left 2 stars because I have to give them credit for their great Shopify integration and really user friendly dashboard and because, once we finally got the process down, the fulfillment was working well. But, until they get rid of their bad apples, prevent the dust from piling up on the products in the warehouse, and hire some customer service reps that actually care, they will continue to get poor feedback.

classification.summary: Jewelry company merchant canceled ShipMonk after 2 years due to severe operational failures including missing inventory, damaged products, and poor customer service, despite praising the dashboard and integration.

content.body:
This is the only real usable shipping software for companies. There are so many options for both domestic but most importantly international.
Many shipping software don't have Fedex as an option, you have to add your own account. Fedex has a new shipping option, International connect plus that is so affordable and quick. (Fedex only allows this if you have your own account if you ship 30k worth of packages a year.) None of my customers say that there are additional brokerage fees yet.
The userface is really easy to use. You can do bulk edits very easily. You can merge same destination shipments (shopify does not let you do that, you need an app). There is a feature that lets you automatically add insurance. You can create a manifest. You can track the packages easily and ask for a refund for the label and insurance claims, and when you process the labels the custom forms populate with the shipping labels.
On my computer the software lags extensively, and there are printer issues that emerged, as in the software would not populate shipping labels and if it did it would not be formatted correctly. I was told to use a different browser and that did fix the printer issue. Dhl is much cheaper on shopify, sometimes as much as $20 and shopify offers free DHL pickup with the purchase of the label.
The really cool feature is what your customers see at checkout. They will see 3 options, cheap, middle, and quickest/most expensive. Then, your international customers will also see what they will pay in taxes and duties. That feature was on my site for the first 2 years. Then, it wasnt. I contacted easyship and they said it was shopify, I contacted shopify and they said it was easyship. I was on a grandfathered plan for both of them and after the hassle and 2 years, i decided to upgrade both plans and that still did not solve it.
The onboarding process was great. Mine was years ago so not sure how it is now.
This is a great software, there are issues and sometimes they are fixed and sometimes they are not. But compared to other shipping software this is really great.

classification.summary: Long-time Easyship user praising the software's shipping options and user interface but frustrated by unresolved technical issues (lag, printer problems) and a lost feature that persisted even after plan upgrades.

content.body:
I have  been working with and for a company that has been utilizing this ReadyStorage work / storage / venue location for quite some time. That said I personally and for quite some time prior to Trevor taking over manager duties at this location I wasn't the biggest fan of this ReadyStorage location.

I don't remember off hand how long it's been since Trevor took the reigns as  Manager of the ReadySpaces Storage Facility at 1185 Campbell, but from day one and  little by little he's been able to take this old dilapidated monster maze of a building  brought it back from the coffin. I work on site or pickup from the facility at least a few times a month so I've def noticed and recognize the work that Trevor has put into the spot. (From Digital App Based Locks to new Carpets in the conference room, new Coffee machines  in the breakroom, are all Trevor's fault.) Prior to Trevor it felt like the place had no soul, and now has more of a community feel. I don't life in the south bay, however if i did I'd look to Readystorage and of course Trevor for all my storage needs.

classification.summary: Long-time user praising the dramatic facility improvements and management transformation under new manager Trevor, with no switching intent or pain points expressed.

content.body: WORST customer service. Shipstation worked great till they switched to Parcel Guard. They ended up costing us thousands of dollars in damages for one shipment. We have double automatic insurance rules on our account, but what they don't advertise is that if you ship more than one package in a shipment, they automatically turn off your insurance. Been back and forth with customer service for months over the issue. Cancelled our account because of how much money we lost. Said they were going to give us a refund, went silent for weeks, stopped responding to our emails then a new support person popped back in after we cancelled and said sorry to see you go and no refund.

classification.summary: The Rustic House cancelled ShipStation after suffering thousands in shipping damage due to undisclosed insurance policy changes (auto-disabling multi-package coverage) and unresponsive customer service that failed to provide promised refunds.

content.body: Terrible integration with Shopify.- If an order is edited (products change, address updated, notes added etc) that edit in Shopify does not flow over to Shipstation. So as a small business owner I need to make the edits twice.- I have two different warehouses and Shopifys split shipping is not supported as an integration with Shipstation.- I have an app on Shopify that makes beautiful, branded packing slips and invoices. But these do not travel over to Shipstation so my packages have this totally mediocre packing slip thats not on-brand for my upscale company.- I sell wholesale on Faire and while Faire and Shopify integrate beautifully when the orders flow through to Shipstation they are all screwed up with testers not registering properly and discounts showing up in a very confusing way. So every time I get a wholesale order through Faire I need to manually email them the packing slip so they can pick and fill correctly.I love my warehouse but wish they did not need to use Shipstation. I feel Shipstation does not care one bit about the business using Shopify, only about the warehouses.

classification.summary: Small business owner frustrated with ShipStation's poor Shopify integration, lack of support for multi-warehouse shipping, inability to use custom packing slips, and problematic third-party marketplace sync, but warehouse-dependent on the platform.

content.body:
This review is about Trevor new the general manager of their facility and readyspaces.

First, why I chose readyspaces. It has warehouse facilties of different sizes but you also have very nice confernce rooms, common kitchen, wifi fir everyone even in the warehouse, mail in and mail out in secure area, video security, two docks, different entry gates, common fork and pallet lifting machines etc......bottom line you get both office and warehosue without paying much..if you don't need big office area and your business does require more inventory space then it has all and that's pretty unique. Now, one advantage when you don't know about the right size of the warehosue as business grows, you can always scale up or scale down based on what's needed.
Readyspaces no wonder is one one for fastest growing companies in a space whoch was already crowded because it is solving problems for many small busineses.

Now about Trevor the new general manager. He has given a soul to this body. He has all the right traits of a great leader who can manage complex operations of a business such as readyspaces. He is there all the time, very reachable and can be reached anytime through any methods..say email, call, in person..this is innstark contrast to the previous one who was very hard to find...I have seen a lot of renovations after he has joined. He has been a positive chnage agent for this place. Restrooms have been upgraded, conference rooms have been remodeled, floor, mats, new entry mechnism via phone...on n on...Many of his qualities , such actively listening to customers and solving their problems proactively, do anything to make customers happy, ( one such example..when one customer came to drop my product, non of the employees or myself was in office)..he called me and got the device and kept in his office...), he is getting investments for the place, and finally..the word soul or a great soul that enlightens the entire place with his hardwork, active engagements, proactive actions and customer collaborations..

classification.summary: Small business owner praising ReadySpaces for flexible, affordable co-warehousing with office space and scaling flexibility, and particularly crediting the new general manager Trevor for dramatically improving operations, customer service, and facilities.

content.body:
I'm extremely happy with our decision to implement ShipHero as our warehouse management solution. From the very beginning, the implementation process was well-organized and enabled us to launch on-time and within budget. The team at ShipHero was incredibly knowledgeable and supportive, tailoring the software to our specific needs and ensuring a smooth transition.
One of the most impressive aspects of ShipHero is its flexibility. Our warehouse environment is complex, with a wide range of products, storage configurations, and unique processes. This software is adapting to our unique requirements, preparing us to optimize our operations and increase efficiency.
The user-friendly interface and intuitive features have made it easy for our team to learn and adopt the software. We expect to see significant improvements in inventory accuracy, order fulfillment speed, and overall productivity in the next few months. I highly recommend ShipHero to businesses looking for a reliable and adaptable warehouse management solution.

classification.summary: Next Adventure is highly satisfied with ShipHero's implementation, flexibility, user-friendly interface, and ability to adapt to their complex warehouse operations.

content.body:
Don't often write reviews but feel an obligation to warn other business owners. Shipbob were very good during the sales process but as soon as we started operating they were useless. We are a UK based business and intended to use shipbob to fulfil EU orders (post brexit).
However, despite following shipbob's UK to EU stock transfer process, our shipments were always fraught with crossborder issues. Our stock transfers were always at least 4 weeks late due to incompetency on shipbobs end. It seemed like shipbob had never onboarded a new customer before, crucial information which cost us thousands of pounds was missed and all the problem solving had to be done from our end due to shipbob often not responding to us or couriers.
We used the service for three months and do not recommend for UK businesses looking to oursource their international fulfilment

classification.summary: UK-based merchant experienced severe delays and poor customer service from ShipBob during EU fulfillment operations, leading to switching after three months.

content.body:
They overcharged me on 6 packages from USA, to USA, all of them weighed less then 5lbs and dimensions small, they overcharged by 550$!!!!!! If my store would have been live and i had 100 orders that they they would have ACH charged me probably over 10k without me being able to do anything about it.
And thats not the worst of it, that was for 2 days guaranteed shipbob express delivery, arrived 8-10 days later.
This was resolved 14 days later, and money credited to my shipbob account.
Not only that but they claim the reason for it was that the dimensions of my products were wrong, well i have an email sent from me 2 weeks before telling them to fix the dimensions of some smaller products, and they said thanks we will fix it, confimed it, but apparently never did.
Why is that ? Because 99% of theire customer service is sitting in a call center in INDIA, and they cant help with jack, they just forward internally, and then you will get another message from a USA based agent that takes theire sweet time because they probably manage like a gazillion accounts.
Not only that i have no gone over to a different 3PL, moved everything delayed my launch lost thousands of dollars due to ShipBob and they have the audacity to charge me a PICK FEE of 1100+$ for shipping out my little invetory i had in there that they charged 360$ for shipping btw and they charged me PICK fee for individual SKUs when i allready had my SKUs boxed up and separated all they needed to do was slap on labels.
I was in here reading reviews before i chose shipbob and i was convinced they were good, so im hoping this post can act as a warning for others like me. I chose to go with Fulfyld instead. DYOR.

classification.summary: Merchant experienced significant overcharges (~$550 on 6 packages), delayed delivery (8-10 days vs 2-day guaranteed), poor customer service from offshore team, and excessive pick fees, prompting them to switch to Fulfyld and delay their store launch.

content.body:
Working in marketing, finding a space that balances functionality with sophistication is paramount, and the ReadySpaces office and warehouse exceeded my expectations on every front.

From the moment you step into the sleek, modern lounge & lobby, you're greeted by an ambiance that speaks of professionalism and luxury, making it an ideal environment for both creative brainstorming sessions and work meetings.

The warehouse facilities are equally impressive, with ample storage space and efficient tools such as forklifts & loading docks to streamline operations. The accessibility and organization of the warehouse make it easy to manage our inventory effectively, which is crucial for maintaining our business.

Beyond the physical space, ReadySpaces provides exceptional amenities and services that cater to every need. From dedicated parking areas & 24-hour restrooms to an on-site cafeteria for lunch breaks & separate private conference room, renters' needs throughout their complete workday can be met. The manager Fil always goes above and beyond to cushion these needs as well!

Overall, ReadySpaces' executive office and warehouse space epitomize both luxury and functionality. It's more than just a place of work; it's a hub of efficiency that empowers ours and other companies' marketing efforts. I would highly recommend it to any business looking to elevate their workspace to the next level.

content.body:
OK, it's time to leave a review as we've been using ShipStation for a few years now.
The fact is that ShipStation is so much better than any other competitor in the space! Seriously - I've tried tons of other apps just in case, and they all fall far short of the functionality and usability of ShipStation. From automations to every day order management, it's just easier in ShipStation.
I've integrated all of our selling channels - Shopify, Etsy, Amazon, TikTok Shop, eBay ... and they all work really well.
Sure, there are issues sometimes - every software has issues - but as a small business with only 2 users, we STILL get an actual support rep to help us with issues. Pretty amazing, really.
I love how easy it is to use USB scales and label printers too - that saves us a ton of time. Sometimes we have to restart the ShipStation client to fix an issue, but it works 99% of the time with no issue and is usually one click to weigh or print labels.
TikTok Shop integration is absolutely amazing! It updates quickly which is so important as TikTok Shop wants us to ship super fast - so any delays are a no-go. The app works seamlessly and doesn't require any difficult configurations, providing API keys, managing things in a separate platform, etc. It just works and is SO welcome!
ShipStation just makes our small business run better. With hundreds of orders a month, there's no way we could do things without it.

classification.summary: Loyal ShipStation customer of several years praising its superior functionality, ease of use, multi-channel integration, responsive support, and quick TikTok Shop updates that enable fast fulfillment for their small business.

content.body:
A Launchpad for Success: ReadySpaces Richmond

ReadySpaces was instrumental in the growth and success of my company over the past two years. As a first time founder, finding the right environment was crucial, and ReadySpaces exceeded all expectations as a nurturing incubator for innovation.

From day one, ReadySpaces impressed. The facilities are modern, well-maintained, and equipped with everything a growing company could need. High-speed internet, spacious meeting rooms, a private offices, loading docks and a shared forklift, ReadySpaces provides a professional yet comfortable workspace.

What truly sets ReadySpaces apart, however, is their unwavering support. The landlord and management team have been nothing short of excellent. Always approachable and responsive, they go above and beyond to ensure that every tenant feels supported. Whether it was troubleshooting a technical issue or accommodating our growing space needs, they were there every step of the way with practical solutions and genuine enthusiasm for our success.

I wholeheartedly recommend ReadySpaces to any startup or growing business looking for more than just a place to work. Here, you'll find a supportive community, top-notch facilities, and a landlord who genuinely cares about your journey. Thank you, ReadySpaces, for playing such a pivotal role in our company's growth and development.

classification.summary: First-time founder praising ReadySpaces for excellent facilities, responsive management support, and instrumental role in company growth over two years.

content.body:
My onboarding experience with ShipBob has been mostly positive, largely thanks to my onboarding manager, Pramod Bora. From the very beginning, Pramod demonstrated excellent and fast communication, making the process smooth and efficient. Whenever an issue arose, it was resolved promptly, showcasing Pramod's commitment to providing top-notch service.
Pramod's explanations were clear and detailed, ensuring that I understood every aspect of the onboarding process. His attention to detail was impressive, catching and addressing potential issues before they became problems.
One of the standout aspects of Pramod's service was his extensive knowledge about Shopify. He skillfully navigated and resolved any issues that surfaced, making the integration seamless and stress-free.
Overall, my onboarding experience with ShipBob, guided by Pramod Bora, has been excellent. The combination of fast communication, quick issue resolution, thorough explanations, and deep Shopify expertise made for a smooth and successful onboarding process.

classification.summary: indomie.us praises ShipBob's onboarding experience, highlighting exceptional communication, quick issue resolution, and deep Shopify integration expertise provided by their onboarding manager Pramod Bora.

content.body:
Easyship has been providing us with shipping solutions for over five years now.
The platform is user-friendly and integrates seamlessly with various e-commerce sites. Shipping rates are customized for each parcel, which is especially valuable for handling non-standard cargo. The intuitive dashboard offers a comprehensive overview of all shipments, tracking information, and delivery statuses. Over the years, Easyship has simplified many procedures to be more customer-friendly, like making the insurance claims process very intuitive (though thankfully, these cases are rare).
The customer support team is responsive and helpful, with special thanks to our customer success manager, Chris, who is our main point of contact for any customizations or improvements we need.
If you want to make your shipping logistics easy and efficient, Easyship is definitely the right platform to choose.

classification.summary: Long-term satisfied customer praising Easyship's user-friendly platform, customized shipping rates for non-standard cargo, intuitive dashboard, and responsive customer support team.

content.body: I filed a complaint against them through Texas Attorney General. Don't rent from them. Big mistake. The idea is fancy but the reality is not. They just enter your suite to deliver packages under "concierge service" and in that way,  the saltbox employees can have an idea about what you store so they can be partner with criminals or talk to people. There are many burglary cases and they just cover it. I have police reports and we lost more than 80k. They are still searching the suspect and suspect is criminal with a long record. They let criminals in and they partner with criminals because there is a huge negligence .Their head office in Georgia so if you want to sue them then you need to find an attorney in GA to sue them and GA protects corps. So they make things complicated. They don't pay for any loss and they don't want to take any responsibility. They just want to wash hands and walk away. Who deals with GA Law?  They just try to attract investors and keep their financial statements shiny. That is it. They have missing master keys to open every single door there etc. I have a statement from former employee. For our case, It turned out that the suspect is  the next-door suite's partner-friend-employee's ex-husband so how come he knew that we had the merchandise? There are 100 suites there why us? No one enters our suite except Saltbox employees.The management and that suite are close friends and the suspect is their facebook friend. They knew him and they let him in. We are not the only ones who suffer. There are many police reports and one of them is the same suspect!!! So I'm sure Texas Attorney General will dig deep. I'm also planning to file complaint for Georgia Attorney General too.

classification.summary: Merchant filed complaint with Texas Attorney General alleging Saltbox negligently allowed burglary of $80k+ in merchandise due to security failures, employee misconduct, and potential collusion with criminals, and is pursuing legal action.

metadata.owner_response:
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We take all reviews seriously and are sorry to hear about your experience. However, we respectfully disagree with the claims made in this review. We pride ourselves on maintaining a safe and secure environment for all our members and their products. Any allegations suggesting otherwise are unfounded and do not reflect the operations and values of Saltbox. In the event of any security incident, we cooperate fully with local authorities and consistently take additional preventative security measures to ensure the safety of our community.

Thank you,
The Saltbox Team

content.body: Accurate, efficient 3PL with a quick-to-respond customer service team. There are more than a few "hidden fees" in your contract so read carefully but in the end it's worth it for knowing your product is being managed well. While the customer service team always answers quickly and kindly, special projects sometimes take a few weeks - so budget time accordingly.

classification.summary: Merchant praised ShipMonk's accuracy and responsive customer service but flagged hidden contract fees and slow timelines for special projects as minor drawbacks to an otherwise reliable 3PL partnership.

content.body: Had a wonderful experience so far with ShipHero. They set up everything super quick and got me to being able to have my inventory tracked and my shipments out! Customer service was top notch! Kyle has been super helpful and responsive. He is very knowledgeable and has been super easy to work with. The system itself very user friendly. I love that I can set up kits within ShipHero without needing to have an extra app in Shopify. I love that I can tell exactly where my inventory goes and it tracks every change! I do wish ( or maybe I haven’t found it) there was an option to see how much inventory was used in the last 30 days. I also love how easy it is to pick and pack orders. Definitely the best system I’ve used so far for warehouse management

content.body:
No problem with the App, but awful, awful customer service.  Stay far away.
The service while using the app was ok. They're a little expensive, and a bit slow. But mostly good enough.
When we decided to stop the service, they were a disaster.  First they failed to get our inventory ready in time for pickup, then they continued to charge us after we closed our account.
If our customer service experience so far is any indication, we'll end up needing to take them to small claims court in order to recover our money.
My advice, if you must use ShipMonk, is to use a credit card that offers a "virtual card" feature, so that you can prevent them from billing your after you close your account.  I have such a feature, but sadly didn't use it.
Better, though, is to avoid them altogether.  There are other 3PL companies that don't have these issues.

classification.summary: Merchant experienced severe customer service failures during offboarding, including missed inventory pickup deadlines and continued billing after account closure, and is actively seeking to switch to alternative 3PL providers.

content.body: Our new 3PL uses this app for shipping labels so unfortunately we are stuck with them at least for now. Since moving to them I've seen a 20% increase in my shipping costs. They use Stamps.com for USPS which has shitty rates and you can't switch to shippo or pirate ship. You can hook up your own accounts if you have them for UPS, Fed Ex, DHL and some others but none that handle USPS. I'm also not in love with the way the app works and the customer service is non-existent. I want to add a rule for the packers to add an insert for orders auto-tagged in shopify as first time buyers but I can't get it to work and I can't get anyone to help. i'm spending way too much of my time on this app.

classification.summary: Merchant locked into ShipStation through their 3PL is experiencing 20% shipping cost increases due to forced Stamps.com USPS rates, lacks control over carrier selection, and receives no customer support for customization needs.

content.body:
ShipStation's integration with Shopify has been a game-changer for our e-commerce businesses helping us greatly streamline our shipping processes.
The initial integration set-up was incredibly user-friendly and after just a few minutes easily syncs with Shopify to update orders and tracking information in real-time. It supports all of the carriers we use (and then some). As a business that ships everything from small USPS priority envelops all the way up to 85 pound ebikes flexibility is key to saving on ever increasing shipping costs.

classification.summary: Bicycle Warehouse praises ShipStation for seamless Shopify integration, real-time order syncing, carrier flexibility, and help reducing shipping costs across their multi-carrier operation.

content.body:
The process to move my e-commerce operations to ShipBob was far easier than I thought it would be.  The onboarding was pretty straight forward, and the dashboard is extremely user friendly. Their platform updates immediately, I was really surprised and impressed with how fast it updates and syncs with whatever ecommerce host you use, we're on Shopify and it's a seamless integration. The SB team designated to get me onboarded has been absolutely fantastic, Pramod is so knowledgeable and helpful he's really made the transition pretty seamless. I can't recommend ShipBob enough.
To anyone who's even considering moving to a 3PL, just do it, sky is the limit and they're here to help get you there.

classification.summary: Kelley Baker Brows praises ShipBob's seamless onboarding process, user-friendly dashboard, fast platform updates, Shopify integration, and exceptional support from their onboarding specialist Pramod.

content.body:
We've been with ShipBob for a few years now and were quite happy in the beginning, but then as we began to expand globally we encountered an inventory syncing issue with Shopify Plus Markets. At first there didn't seem to be a solution and we were worried we'd have to leave ShipBob and find and alternative solution.
However, they really came through for us. Our account manager, Savi, and Simon from their dev team put together a custom solution to solve our integration problem. It took them some time and effort to develop and test the solution, but it has made our life so much easier and we're super stoked to be staying with ShipBob now that our integration issues are solved.
They really came through for us in the end. In fact, they came through for us in the beginning too. When we first came to ShipBob it was a rush onboarding because our current 3PL at the time had just sold to another company and it was a total gong show. We needed to get rehomed fast and ShipBob managed to onboard us fast when we really needed it.
I would highly recommend ShipBob. They have been awesome for us.

classification.summary: Canadian merchant with multi-year relationship praises ShipBob for solving a critical Shopify Plus Markets inventory syncing issue through custom development and exceptional customer support, resulting in renewed loyalty.

content.body:
It takes a lot for us to write a negative review but Shipbob has more than earned one. We used them to handle overseas fulfillment (Australia). However, they were frequently taking up to a week to process and ship orders, despite initial assurances that orders would be shipped within 24 hours--same day if placed before 11AM local time. Then, after they finally got around to shipping the orders, they often used budget couriers that then took 2-3 weeks to deliver.
Not only this, but their shipping rates were sky high. In fact it turned out to be cheaper, and often faster, to fulfill Australian orders from the USA. Their support is awful too. There is no way to get immediate assistance and you are forced to open a ticket and wait, at times, several days for a response. I have used three fulfillment services in the past and Shipbob is by far the absolute worst.

classification.summary: Australian fulfillment customer switching from ShipBob due to unacceptable delays (up to a week to process/ship), high costs, slow delivery couriers, and poor support response times—found it cheaper and faster to fulfill from the USA instead.

content.body: Shipstation made it extremely easy for us to ship many orders at once with only a few clicks. If your business is like ours and you often ship more than 20 orders a day, I would highly recommend Shipstation. Once item weights were inputed into Shopify, and automation rules were set in Shipstation, we were able to save at least an hour of work each day in the shipping process. Not to mention the low rates Shipstation offers. By the end of the 30-day trial, I was relieved to have found this app. : )

classification.summary: High-volume shipper praising ShipStation for its ease of use, automation capabilities, time savings of ~1 hour per day, and competitive pricing.

content.body: Setup was very easy even with additional marketpalces like TikTok Shop and Etsy importing orders. Highly recommended for startups looking to scale without the overhead of renting and staffing a warehouse. The implementation team was also great to work with. Shoutout to Maverick for making everything a breeze! No more dealing with label printers and packaging out of my 1-bedroom apartment :)

content.body:
I quite like it. Much better experience than buying labels directly from UPS etc...
There is one thing I would really appreciate if you improved it:
It would be great if the customs declaration could be made more 'versatile'. It seems there is no way to enter "Gift" or any other type of shipment. It is always 'Merchandise'. Also the Item description is way too short. for example, sometimes it is necessary to add "no commercial value" or "warranty replacement" etc...So a few more lines would be great.
Keep up the good work!

classification.summary: Merchant is satisfied with Easyship but requests more flexibility in customs declaration options (shipment types beyond 'Merchandise') and longer item description fields for international shipping.

content.body:
Update: The only response I've gotten from ShipStation was because of this review. I still haven't heard back from anyone about my problem in most of a week. Seems they care more about how they look than how they actually do their jobs.
It keeps getting more expensive, while the service keeps getting worse.
I've been three days without the ability to create labels, and I still can't figure out if any of the support staff (who appear to be farmed out to a 3rd party now) have even used the software before.
Not only did my fees DOUBLE for using my own carrier accounts, but the service and support are 1/10th of what they used to be.
This is the worst way to spend your money. Find something else.
Im cancelling and moving to Ordoro.
Update: After 2 weeks of outage my issue was finally resolved and we were able to create labels again with the carrier we have the best agreement with.
We lost ~$3000 in increased shipping costs in this window of time. Shipstation was gracious enough to provide us with a $400 credit to make up for this....
Responses were slow, and we were more or less left completely in the dark on what was going on, or what discoveries were made. We were never provided with a reason for the outage.
We are still working to migrate away from ShipStation at this point.

classification.summary: Canadian merchant is actively cancelling ShipStation due to doubled fees, service outages lasting 2+ weeks, poor support responsiveness, and insufficient compensation, and is migrating to Ordoro.

content.body: Degraded quality of platform, and MIA chat support for 3+ year account. Easyship at the beginning of my ecom startup was great, had supportive account reps and overall chat support was great (shoutout to Tracie Harper, 10/10 chat agent). Over the past year, I noticed lots of admin changes which lead to huge changes to support and overall the reach with my account. Every week there would be some issue caused with an order being misplaced, improper address verification, and lots and lots of fees being tagged onto my account due to incorrect address validation done in communication with Shopify and Easyship. Recently, I decided to give up on this platform and exercise my options for a dedicated carrier account. Fortunately, I was provided with one and made the right decision to shift my logistics over to Shipstation and Zonos fulfillment. Both platforms have had nothing but great feedback, and these platforms are progressing in such a great way that I am at the point of hopping away. Easyship was once an "easy" platform, but they have become so difficult to work with that I would recommend new small businesses to use Shipstation as your 3rd party fulfillment. Shipstation support is tremendously better and they are quick to respond to you. I am not at a massive scale but being respected still by a platform like Shipstation shows they care. Do not use this app, stay away and go to Shipstation for domestic and use Zonos for international orders!

classification.summary: Long-time Easyship customer actively switched to Shipstation and Zonos due to degraded platform quality, poor support responsiveness, recurring order and address validation errors, and unexpected fees.

content.body:
My company has been a tenant at WareSpace in Downers Grove for the past four months, and we couldn't be more satisfied. It's an excellent concept for small businesses and startups seeking affordable warehouse space. The management is exceptional; Giorgiana, in particular, is always friendly and attentive to everyone's needs. The facilities boast impressive amenities like free-to-reserve meeting rooms, a kitchen area with complimentary coffee, and utilities included in the rent. Additionally, the staff organizes monthly events for tenant networking, which I've found invaluable for establishing connections.
In summary, I highly recommend WareSpace to anyone needing warehouse space. I encourage scheduling a visit to see firsthand how fantastic this place is.

content.body: The concept is great, however, the execution at this location is less than desirable. I was unfortunately very disappointed when new management came in and after 8 months of everything being fine, it suddenly was not. I made the mistake of adding my bank account for automatic drafts and what a HUGE mistake this has been. They charge my account whenever they feel like it with absolutely no explanation or follow up on their behalf. This is completely unacceptable. Just beware that management will not care to follow up with any of your concerns. They are terrible. I had to end my lease after only one year because I will not accept this type of treatment. I found my own warehouse with a loading dock and room for a showroom for the same amount as what I was paying here and triple the square footage. I wanted to love this but it is a big no no to leave your clients hanging with no response month after month.

classification.summary: Merchant terminated their one-year lease with Saltbox due to poor management, unresponsive customer service, and unauthorized billing practices, and has already secured an alternative warehouse with better terms.

content.body: Our company called in to inquire about warehousing and office space and left our information with the Cubework. Ramon, the site manager, had reached out to us and provided us with all the info about the West Sacramento location and we were interested. We toured the site and submitted our application which was a very streamlined process. Our warehousing space has proven to be very beneficial and convenient for us, a growing business. It allows us to focus on revenue driving tasks rather than property management issues and this is where they shine. Ramon takes care of any issues going on at the site and handles them fast! A dock door is broken? Handled. Any issues with the site? Handled. Amenities like Wi-Fi, receiving packages or a client showing up outside of our hours? Handled. Ramon has everything dialed in so we can focus on our business. 10/10 would recommend Cubework to anyone wanting to grow and scale their business without dealing with old school commercial real estate practices that just aren't any good for today's businesses.

classification.summary: Growing ecommerce merchant is satisfied with Cubework's West Sacramento co-warehousing space and responsive management, allowing them to focus on revenue generation rather than facility operations.

content.body:
Our company just finish on-boarding with Alissa who is the Senior Solutions Specialist. She is an all-star! We have gone through on-boarding with other programs that our company used previously and this is by far the best experience that we have ever had. Alissa is very knowledgeable, patient and genuinely kind. We were sad once our on-boarding process ended because we wish that we could continue working with her!
Ship Hero has been the best system that we've been able to find for our company. Alias mapping SKUs, assembly bundles and other kits have been a breeze to set up and manage. Dynamic inventory has been a huge change to our system but it has truly helped us grow and track inventory more efficiently. Integrations with Amazon and Shopify work very well. Inventory adjustments push almost instantaneously to our sales channels. We are very happy with our experience so far. We only wish that Ship Hero had a manufacturing function so that we could use the program for all of our needs!

classification.summary: Poppy & Pout Wholesale is very satisfied with ShipHero's onboarding experience, inventory management capabilities, and integrations, though they wish the platform had manufacturing functionality to consolidate all their operational needs.

content.body:
If you are in the market for an Australian based 3PL, avoid Shipbob at all costs if you value your time, money or sanity!
Short summary of our experience:- SO MANY lost parcels due to use of cheap carriers.- Once parcels are lost, the overseas support team have no idea how to resolve the issue and they build up with no resolution, leading to upset customers.- Overcharged based on the agreed pricing which they refused to acknowledge for approx 4 x weeks. They finally folded and agreed to credit us the over charged amounts over 6 weeks ago and the credit has still not been received after 6 follow ups.- Increased our pricing without prior notice or our consent.- Storage is double the market rates.- Inventory receipts overcharged (over $450 for 6 x pallets).- Any issues raised take weeks to resolve with multiple follow ups.
Dealing with Shipbob was one of the most frustrating experiences our business has encountered over the last 3 years. Since moving to a new 3pl we have been very happy we are pleased Shipbob no longer consumes hours of our day.

classification.summary: Australian merchant switched away from ShipBob due to lost parcels, poor support, overcharging, unannounced price increases, excessive storage costs, and slow issue resolution.

content.body:
I have used Shipstation for 4+ years. I liked Shipstation despite its quirks. Two weeks ago they overfilled my Stamps.com by $800 account due to their system error when we tried to ship and refill our account. When your Stamps.com account balance is too low, a window will pop up to refill it. We recharged with $200, or so we thought, but we got the prompt again that we didn't have enough balance and we needed to purchase more. Did it. .system error - same thing. Little did we know their purchases were delayed by 20 to 30 minutes and each time we pressed the buy button, it was purchasing more shipping and creating a shipment, but not showing the shipment or updating the balance in our account. So 5 transactions, $200 each -$800 more than needed. I only found this with our credit card transaction review. We called them and they refused to refund me without canceling my Stamps.com account. They were very interested in how this happens, so guessing they were debugging their system error. We didn't need $1000 shipping, so after we reached out to them and tried to resolve it, we contested with our credit card for $600 of that. Shipstation was very quick to shut my account down. If there is anything I can say about Shipstation is that system of shutting off a customer's account is very quick and efficient. Good job. After we realized our account was shut down, we called back in and they said the only way to get reactivated was to bill back the $600. Without options and needing to ship, we caved and said go ahead. Start the clock, it took them another 7 days before we could ship. This service feels like they really don't want me as a customer or think overcharging and holding hundreds of dollars of customer money is good business. Will I recommend Shipstation - No Way - something is broken over there. did I before this? Yes. I wasted over 6+ hours of my time over 7 days resolving Shipstation's screws ups and bureaucracy in getting my account turned on. I don't know if there are better options at this point, but you can be sure I am scheduling time to figure it out. If you want to count: 7 calls over 7 days to get this resolved. 6+ hours of my time. On several calls with primary support, the agent said they couldn't resolve my issues - Only the people at XXX number could resolve this. Then when I called the XXX number, they said they couldn't resolve the issue either and to call back the original number. I asked for a supervisor, said there is none and to call back the regular number. Did that, waited on hold again asked for a supervisor, agent said no for 15 minutes until he spent the time reviewing my case and asking all the same questions. Never got to speak with a supervisor. I run a business - like all people who have a Shopify account. Stuff happens, but when it does having your account shut down for a system-critical service is a real issue. If you do use their services, have a plan in place for when you have issues and expect multiple-day downtimes. Expect no apologies. Expect no one to take responsibility. Expect a lot of broken promises and wasted follow-up. Also, they sent me a survey to ask how they did. After starting, I realized it would have taken me over 30 minutes to complete it. I opted to spend that time here to let potential customers know about my experience. Update 6/23/2022 - Shipstation/Stamps management reached out to me by phone/email to understand this issue better and see what went wrong after seeing this review. They are going to look through their support strings and try to figure out what went wrong here and try to make it right. There is someone at the other end of the line here listening at least.
Update 10/23/2023 -We have used shipstation as a backup and for very specific shipping needs, but find that shopify rates are the same or better in most instances. If you use UPS and UPS misses a $200 overnight first - you can try to get a refund from Shipstation, but they have a weird form, zero follow up and say that if UPS approves the refund, we will see a refund - just did an quick check against our and I have never seen a refund - at least 3, likely 5 submission this year.  Seems like they give you a place to push buttons with zero follow up.   They don't have any paper trail.  Still don't recommend these guys - they will stay as a backup for us unless they don't like this review.
If you are another vendor, don't reach out to me to solicit based on this.  I am covered.

classification.summary: Long-time ShipStation customer experienced a system error that resulted in $800 in unauthorized charges, faced poor refund handling and account suspension, and is now actively seeking alternatives after 6+ hours of frustration resolving the issue.

content.body:
They lost my inventory!
ShipBob is terrible and I will be moving to another 3PL provider ASAP.
I was hesitant what with the negative reviews here and on TrustPilot, but the sales team and onboarding process gave me confidence. Boy was I wrong and should've gone with my first instinct to avoid them like the plague.
On a single receiving order they managed to create chaos and have nearly destroyed my business.
Some of the specific issues that are still not resolved.
1. On receiving an order they incorrectly weighed items so the weight in the system was 5x the actual weight, there is no way to change this data. We use CartBob (another Shopify app) for dynamic shipping rates and the impact of this is that customers were overcharged 2-3x for shipping. Many potential customers rightly dropped off and frequent customers complained that we were ripping them off on shipping (fair point). I raised the issue 6 days ago, was assured the items would be rescanned 5 days ago and still nothing has been fixed. They also had the audacity to say they were going to charge me for the time of rescanning the affected products, despite it clearly being their fault.
2. 360 units of SKUs were deleted from their system in error. The stock is sitting there as I can see the history, but the system is saying it's 0. I raised with support and after 3 days they have not resolved the issue. They just so happened to be the highest selling SKUs and so the marketing dollars spent on driving traffic is wasted.
I can accept that issues happen, but the most important thing is taking responsibility and correcting them in a timeframe that minimises the damage to their customers business.
When things go well, it works well, however if there is any issue they are impossible to reach and the response time is disgraceful.
The support team are incredibly slow and operate with an arrogance, disdain and severe lack of empathy for the customers.
---UPDATE---After 7 days they have told me that they can't find the 360 items of inventory, despite there being a visible audit history in the system of the inventory being received on their end. What's particularly interesting is all inventory changes are tied to a specific users full name, but in the case of deleting the inventory it was tied to a shared user name ("Junior 2") so the actual person responsible is not identifiable. This gives an insight into how their internal operations work.
A 3PL that loses that amount of inventory cannot and should not be trusted.
I have a simple business, only 26 SKUs, everything lightweight and nothing bulky.
They are amateurs and completely negligent.
Do not trust them with your business.

classification.summary: Merchant is actively switching 3PL providers due to critical inventory management failures (lost/deleted SKUs, incorrect weight data) and poor support responsiveness that damaged their business.

content.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

I recently had the pleasure of renting a space from Ready Spaces for my project, and I must say, it was an exceptional experience from start to finish. This was absolutely the best place for my project!!

The first thing that impressed me was the variety of spaces they offered. From the well-appointed conference rooms to the cozy break room, Ready Spaces had it all.

One of the standout features of Ready Spaces is their incredibly versatile contracts. They understand that every project is unique, and they tailor their rental agreements to fit your specific requirements. This flexibility made the entire renting process hassle-free over the competition.

Security is a top priority at Ready Spaces, and that gave me tremendous peace of mind. Knowing that the property was always under security ensured the safety of my equipment and valuable assets. It's clear that they take the security of their tenants seriously.

Moreover, the fellow tenants at Ready Spaces were incredibly friendly. It created a welcoming atmosphere that made me look forward to coming to work each day. Everyone there was not just professional but also genuinely pleasant to interact with.

I can't say enough about the manager, Jamie. She went above and beyond to make my experience at Ready Spaces outstanding. Her professionalism, accommodation, and ability to handle any difficult situation with ease were truly commendable. Jamie's assistance made my time at Ready Spaces even more enjoyable.

In conclusion, if you're looking for a rental space that offers versatility, security, and a friendly environment, Ready Spaces is the place to be. My project flourished in this exceptional environment, and I wholeheartedly recommend it to anyone in need of a top-notch rental space. Thank you, Ready Spaces, for providing such a fantastic space and experience!

content.body:
As a small business owner, I know firsthand that sometimes you're riding high, and other times, you're scrambling to keep up with demand. That's where ReadySpaces came to my rescue.

1. Lifesaving Flexibility: When the pressure's on, you can't afford to get stuck in a long-term lease. ReadySpaces understands that. Their month-to-month leasing was a game-changer for my business during a hectic period. I could set up shop quickly and scale up or down as needed.

2. Community Connection: What truly impressed me was the sense of community at ReadySpaces. They offer fantastic communal spaces where you can watch TV, enjoy a meal, and have impromptu discussions with fellow entrepreneurs. It's not just a workspace; it's a thriving community that helps you thrive.

3. Professional Meeting Areas: During my crunch time, I needed a place to have important client meetings and brainstorm with my team. ReadySpaces had me covered with professional meeting spaces that made a big impression on clients and facilitated productive discussions.

4. Turnkey Solution: ReadySpaces is a turnkey solution for businesses like mine. No need to worry about setting up internet, phone, or Wi-Fi. Just bring a lock to secure your unit, complete some straightforward paperwork, and you're good to go. They handle the nitty-gritty so you can focus on what matters most.

5. Supportive Team: The ReadySpaces team was a lifeline. Quick responses, helpful advice, and solutions that actually worked. It felt like they were as invested in my success as I was.

So, if you're a small business owner facing crunch time, I can't recommend ReadySpaces enough. They were my lifeline when I needed space that was flexible, hassle-free, and supported my growth while providing a vibrant community and professional meeting areas. Trust me, they've got your back when it counts the most.

classification.summary: Small business owner praising ReadySpaces for flexible month-to-month leasing, supportive team, community amenities, and ability to quickly scale space during periods of high demand.

content.body:
As a start-up in the E-commerce space, Ready Space provided exactly what I needed at a competitive rate.  This is without a doubt the best option for anyone who is looking to start or grow their business.

1) Lease and size flexibility -  Their lease contracts are much more flexible than other traditional options out there and you can easily move to a different size unit in the same warehouse if needed.  There are a lot of uncertainties with starting a business, the last thing I wanted to do was to lock myself in a long-term lease with a traditional warehouse as the business could easily grow or sink and I would be stuck.

2) Turn key option and other useful amenities - The warehouse is move-in ready, I didn't have to worry about any renovations.  It is modern and well-kept.  There were loading ramps and docks for any sized truck.   There are communal pallet jacks and a forklift so I didn't have to spend money buying it.  Pricing includes all utilities as well so I didn't have to worry about Wifi, Electricity or the garbage bill.  All of these options lowered my start-up cost greatly.

3) Safety - This is in a very safe area with plenty of private parking.  There are 24 hr surveillance and lots of other businesses in the warehouse so I didn't have to worry about someone breaking in even if I was gone for a long time.  There is an area that is easily accessible for shipping and receiving which was extremely important to me as an e-commerce store.  I could leave my inbound/outbound packages there and leave early if I needed to without having to worry about it getting stolen.

Ready space provides you with everything you need from a traditional warehouse without all the extra costs and headaches.  Would recommend 10/10.

classification.summary: Startup e-commerce merchant praising ReadySpaces for flexible lease terms, turnkey warehouse amenities, low startup costs, and security features that enabled business growth without long-term commitment risk.

DO NOT USE THIS COMPANY TO FULFILL YOUR PRODUCTS!! I have used shipbob for over 2 years now and thought they were decent, minor issues but nothing crazy. The customer support is ABSOLUTELY TERRIBLE, I found a new warehouse within 1 day and have been trying to get shipbob to ship my inventory from their warehouse to my new fulfilment centre AND THEY HAVE NOT EVEN SHIPPED IT YET! content.body:
We've shipped 5,350 orders using this terrible software and this is our review. I write this review as we currently have 250+ orders that need to be shipped. We have countless upset customers emailing us to inquire about their late and unreceived orders. We haven't been able to bulk ship orders because Shipstation's software is down. USPS altered their shipping services 11 days ago on July 9th. Because of this slight change at USPS all of our orders that will use USPS can not be bulk shipped. The only options we have now is to either ship orders one at a time or to use another more expensive shipping service like UPS and FedEx which will cost us hundreds of dollars. We've tried contacting Shipstation via their chat support 3 times in 3 days for almost a week. Each time we've spoken to a new lazy, slow and poorly trained agent that seems to be from a foreign country. They've waste our time and failed to solve this issue or to tell us when it would be fixed. Each time we're asked to send screenshots and are asked a bunch of useless and generic questions about our issue. We've also spoken to their "premium" phone support 2 times in 2 days who've told us Shipstation has known about this software error for many days and has yet to fix it. When we told them how frustrated we were about this and how long it was taking to be fixed we were given a typical corporate nonsense response that sounded something like "our engineers are aware of this issue and are working very hard to resolve it very soon."
In our opinion Shipstation is:1. A poorly designed software that constantly breaks and causes us slow shipping times and headaches.2. Is too complicated and hard to learn and use. Simple tasks require multiple steps.3. Has poorly trained, slow and useless support that don't know how to troubleshoot and fix their own software.4. Is too expensive considering the low quality service it offers.5. Has pathetically slow progression when it comes to fixing issues with their software.

classification.summary: Astro Ridge has 250+ orders backed up due to ShipStation's software failure to integrate with USPS changes and is actively seeking alternatives like UPS/FedEx to avoid further delays and customer dissatisfaction.

content.body:
We have been using EasyShip for nearly 3 years. During that time we have had significant challenges (on normal features and while testing beta-features) due to internal and external factors. Our Account Managers (we've had several during that time) have been responsive when these challenges arose and were solution oriented. My advice to anyone considering this service is to learn about setting up shipping rules to best serve your business and customer. If done correctly and thoroughly, everyone's lives are made simple.
While there is a customer service team, any orders sent using a limited or basic tracking courier they're unable to aid in tracking down missing/lost parcels. Pay careful attention to label prices you're paying and what the app is charging your customer.
Overall, EasyShip is a good service with simple integration with our Shopify store.

classification.summary: Long-term Easyship customer acknowledges service challenges but credits responsive account management and notes that proper setup of shipping rules and careful cost monitoring are key to success with the platform.

content.body:
I've been using Easyship for approximately 5 years to send parcels to the US and other international destinations in partnership with a third party broker that carries parcels from Canada to the US for deposit with USPS, UPS, etc. A great deal has improved over the years, but even from the start, Easyship offered the most accurate volumetric rate based system that helped us save so much time by not needing to manually enter the weight or dimensions of every single parcel. With respect to more recent changes, Easyship now has its own in-house insurance system which is easier and faster to use than anything that preceeded it. I'm very happy with Easyship and forsee using it for years to come.
One last thought: i'm hearing impaired and am grateful for the ability to seek support via email instead of being forced into phone calls.

classification.summary: Long-term Canadian customer praising Easyship's accurate volumetric rate system, improved insurance, and accessibility support via email.

content.body:
I have edited my review in July 2023Previously i thought"This makes my life so much more simple.Firstly, the support I have had is really good. An initial meeting was set up with someone and she supported me all the way.I have very complex settings to enable me to automate my shipping method and weight, and the app is very customisable to your needs.I can easily download all of my shipments before I confirm them, so I can count them and make sure they are correct first. It enables you to easily flag express orders and certain types of orders.You can also use it to print out all of your packing slips, which is very useful. It also tells you if you have already printed them.I used to use Royal mail click and drop, but this makes it so much easier.It is working really well."
NOW HOWEVER I have had a number of shipments that I cant create a label for. I get strange error messages. Support are unable to help. Unfortunately unless it is a simple error, support have to escalate the query and usually this means you NEVER hear back with a solution. I usually give up at this point and have to go direct to Click and Drop and create a label there. On one occasion I was told i would have to wait 2 weeks!! To which I said to not bother as I needed it sorted by end of day.FOR WEEKS I have had an issue with Ship Station Connect disconnecting, making it an utter nightmare to get my labels printed. Nobody is sorting it out. They tell you to do basic stuff which I have done and have had no effect, as I had thought. I keep getting stupid emails saying "let us know if you are still having issues".It is really slow now too and keeps glitchingPlus in shopify it says your app is unsupported now and needs updating. What is going on?I give up and am NOW LOOKING TO LEAVE TO A DIFFERENT APP.

classification.summary: UK-based merchant is actively leaving ShipStation due to persistent technical issues (label creation errors, app disconnections, slowness), unresponsive support, and long resolution times, and plans to switch to a different shipping app.

content.body:
Honestly the transition to a 3PL was not easy nor quick. However since we found Shipmonk we felt confident Shipmonk could take us to the next level as far as of fulfillment and storage.
Our Happiness Engineer is amazing, she's trustworthy, she assist us with any issues that may arise. I'm happy to say the communication we have developed is hard to find.
In the beginning we would meet weekly until we got the hang of things, then monthly to now only if needed.
Their SOPs are within regular expectations, nothing crazy or out of this world.
I would definitely recommend using their services, I do recommend to do some research and ask as many questions, so there are no unmet expectations or surprises.

classification.summary: Merchant is satisfied with ShipMonk's fulfillment capabilities, praising their responsive Happiness Engineer and quality communication support that helped them successfully transition to using the 3PL.

content.body: Everything about them is amazing! From my first sales call, to every onboarding call since, Shipmonk has been the most seamless and comforting aspect of my journey in e-commerce.  They are kind, helpful, and extremely knowledgeable, and they always put me at ease.  I always look forward to calls with Shipmonk reps (Shoutout to Pascalle and Charli) because I know they'll always be able to help me get my stuff sorted out especially when it seems a bit overwhelming.  Can't recommend them enough!

content.body:
I wish I could leave zero stars! Beware! The damage has already been done to our business. Do yourself a favor and look elsewhere unless you are looking for a shipping company that cannot ship!! 10+ days for fulfillment during the holidays, mispicked damage and lost parcels, inventory processing delays, the list goes on. They use OSM worldwide for their affordable pricing plan which still ends up being $14-20 for packages that we would normal ship first class for under $6. They claim free dunnage and packed with care but we had to submit claims every day for damaged deliveries.
Our customers were furious and our customer service team worked over time by the damage to our business has been immeasurable. We are no on day 12 of waiting for our inventory to ship back to us. AGAIN THIS IS A SHIPPING COMPANY THAT CANNOT SHIP!
Customer service is outsourced, slow, and just “escalates” with no resolution. I can keep going on all day with the immeasurable headache shipbob has caused.
Their management is pathetic. Their warehouse employees are lazy. This company will not last if only the truth got out.
BEWARE
*UPDATEOur inventory was returned by ship Bob. Everything was thrown into boxes, including the glass products. We filed a claim and waited 120 days they took zero responsibility and denied our claim. Shocker. We are filing a lawsuit.

classification.summary: Timeless Organics Skin Care is actively leaving ShipBob due to critical failures in fulfillment speed, package damage, inventory mishandling, poor customer service, and denied damage claims that caused significant business harm.

content.body:
We transitioned to Easyship from Shipstation and it's made shipping so much easier for our warehouse staff. The setup was very simple and interface is very easy to navigate.
The one problem we had was you had to create a shipping rule literally for every thing whereas Easyship natively handles most of the heavy lifting for you with it's rate shopping to easily help find the cheapest shipping option as well as the ideal package size (previously in Shipstation we had to create 10+ shipping rules to get this to run anywhere close to Easyship's automation).
We also thoroughly enjoy having the live chat customer service if there's any challenges along the way as well as our customer service manager, Conor, has been invaluable in helping us overcome a couple small challenges in the set up process. Conor was extremely quick to reply with any of our email inquiries and helped us bring a resolution quickly from the Easyship team.
We give them 5 stars all the way for living up to their name, Easyship, as it's saved so many headaches for our shipping team to get our shipments out quickly and easily.

classification.summary: The Grind Athletics switched from Shipstation to Easyship and praises the superior automation for rate shopping and package sizing, ease of use, and responsive customer service.

content.body:
We formerly were using Stamps.com before making the transition to Easyship. They have truly built an easy to navigate interface and make it extremely simple to use, effective way for our warehouse team to ship hundreds of orders every day.
We've been using them for over 2 years and love the 1 on 1 business relationship we've developed with our customer service managers to help us navigate any issues that develop as well as learn about new features coming to the platform.  Our first CSM helped us establish the process of creating pick lists to help picking/packing become so much faster and efficient.
We definitely recommend Easyship to all businesses looking to simple their mass batching and shipping processes. They truly live up to their name by making this an easy daily process for our warehouse staff.  Thank you to Easyship, and our CSM rep, Conor, for always helping overcome any obstacle that comes our way and and make shipping our products a breeze.

classification.summary: Satisfied long-term customer who switched from Stamps.com to Easyship and credits the intuitive interface, efficient warehouse processes, and dedicated customer service for their success shipping hundreds of orders daily.

content.body: Our onboarding rep, David Lowthorp, went above and beyond for us. During a chaotic time for our company - my first time using a 3PL and also our company getting ready to launch - David made sure everything on the Shipmonk side of things was seamless...he actually made it fun! I was a bit daunted by the process, but having him walk me through it, at times, step-by-step, and at all hours of the day, made things incredibly manageable and made me confident in my ability to manage future inventory deliveries and confident in our companies ability to provide a swift delivery and excellent experience to our customers. I couldn't be happier with the onboarding experience at Shipmonk, and specifically, David. If it's any indication of whats to come I know we are in excellent hands and made the right choice with this 3PL. I will definitely be recommending them to all of my friends and colleagues in need of one.

classification.summary: Merchant praised ShipMonk's exceptional onboarding experience and David Lowthorp's personalized support that helped them confidently launch their first 3PL partnership.

content.body:
transitioning to a warehouse from in house fulfillment was a really huge step for my business. searched for a long time to find the right fulfillment center that checked all the boxes for Wild Woods.
initially it was a huge learning curve for me - im not very tech savvy so some of the onboarding process was challenging for me to understand.luckily I was able to contact a shipmonk team member very easily every time I would call in. they answered all of my questions, screen-shared with me,  & walked me through certain processes I didnt full understand.
recently we have decided to sell on amazon, and I was worried it was going to be a really difficult process to have our invnentory split between shipmonk and FBA but after speaking with customer service at shipmonk they explained everything and the process is really quite easy. shipmonk is familiar with amazons receiving guidelines and all I have to do is submit an order and add to the notes that its going to an amazon warehouse and they literally take care of the rest. I am really impressed!!
matthew scherer has helped me immensley, he is very in depth when explaining things- always gets back to me right away with email and has just been a blessing in helping me anytime I have needed it!  amanda jones,  & sarah box have also been a huge help in various requests I have called in about.
I am really happy with our choice to go with shipmonk. now that I understand the system on a better level, everything is easier and more fluid.
thanks guys!

classification.summary: Merchant transitioned to ShipMonk for 3PL fulfillment and is highly satisfied with their customer service, technical support, and ability to manage split inventory between ShipMonk and Amazon FBA.

content.body: Shipbob is an absolute nightmare.  I have never dealt with a company with such poor customer service.  It takes weeks to resolve issues that are mistakes on their end.  We have had consistent issues with them entering significantly incorrect product dimensions and weights.  This has resulted in customers being quoted shipping rates 5-8 times higher than they should be.  To make it worse, their customer service is taking weeks to fix each issue.  The most recent issue took 6 weeks, just to get them to correct incorrect product dimensions (customers can't edit product dimensions or weights).  For anyone considering using Shipbob, I would strongly encourage you to look at alternative options.

classification.summary: Merchant is actively seeking alternatives due to ShipBob's persistent errors in product dimensions/weights causing inflated customer shipping quotes and weeks-long resolution times for customer service issues.

content.body: AVOID SHIPBOB LIKE THE PLAGUE! They catfish you wish low shipping fees (at first my average cost to ship sat at around $7), and storage fees - however every time they receive a new shipment they give a NEW pick spot and NEVER consolidate, so you pay triple in my case in some instances.... the method of working out shipping fees magically changed after 6 months, doubling my shippings. Despite this - You cannot get your stock out without crazy costs and they will not allow you to pick up, you must pay to have it shipped at their exorbitant costs (which is the reason to move away in the first place). Customer service is awful (based in India and impossible to have a phone call with anyone. They advertise there is a chat service and number, but these are rarely manned and the chatbot is useless. I know 4 other brands who have fallen for Shipbob's over-promising/under-delivering sales pitch and all have either moved or are in the process of leaving. The biggest mistake this financial year was onboarding ShipBob.The one thing they do well is pick/ship quickly.

classification.summary: Australian merchant actively switching away from ShipBob due to deceptive pricing practices, non-consolidated storage leading to inflated costs, poor customer service, and inability to retrieve inventory without excessive fees, though acknowledging fast fulfillment speed.

content.body:
There is some good, and a lot of bad with this company.
Good: They ship orders promptly. They integrate with a lot of different ecommerce service providers.
Bad: Their shipping prices are ABSURDLY high. Shipping shoes from Texas to Colorado with ShipBob is $46. With Shopify Shipping it's $10. I cannot ask my customers to pay that much, so I have been eating about $18 of shipping on every sale. Storage - they talk about $40 per pallet and a price for shelves, without telling you how much can fit on a shelf. I can fit all my inventory on 22 pallets. So should be $880, right? But they are storing it on shelves and charging me $2600 a month. When a customer returns an item, shipBob takes 10 days AFTER IT ARRIVES, to even acknowledge arrival, much less inspect the product. Customers cannot wait that long for refunds or exchanges so I am processing those all manually early, taking the risk of damaged product. They use sketchy shipping subcontractors that leave your product sitting in a warehouse for a month on the regular. They lose packages in their own warehouse before pickup and blame the carrier. I am moving all my inventory out as we speak.

classification.summary: VeloVetta is actively migrating inventory out of ShipBob due to prohibitively high shipping costs ($46 vs $10 competitor rates), opaque and excessive storage fees ($2600/month for shelf inventory), slow returns processing (10+ days), and unreliable fulfillment partner performance.

content.body: Seriously the worst fulfillment company on the planet! At first, they attract you with low shipping fees and suddenly they increase the shipping fees! At first items were charged based on actual weight, the shipping fee is 7$.  now they are charged on dimensional weight! An magic wand shipping costs like $70!!!! How ridiculous is that!!! Their US shipping fee is a lot higher than direct ship from China. 17USD for 300G , I do not know how many business can be profitable on this.   Shame!

classification.summary: Merchant is actively dissatisfied with ShipBob due to sudden shipping fee increases and unfavorable dimensional weight pricing that makes their business unprofitable, comparing negatively to direct shipping from China.

content.body:
We chose Easyship as a way to send international shipments. For a year we selected UPS services that showed the recipient would pay taxes, yet at the end of the year we got billed for all of these taxes.
When contacting UPS directly, we were told this was due to the declaration statement being incorrect. This is something Easyship provides electronically so we weren't aware until all of the charges came through at the end of the year.
We've contacted Easyship but they've been incredibly unhelpful, basically telling us that it isn't there fault, when clearly it is.
We now use UPS directly, as the small benefit of using Easyship is greatly outweighed by the numerous charges incurred by using them.

classification.summary: UK merchant switched away from Easyship after being unexpectedly billed for international shipping taxes due to incorrect declaration statements provided by Easyship, citing poor customer support and now using UPS directly.

content.body:
Ready Spaces has been an amazing place to launch my business. The warehouse facility is old and needed some serious TLC, which the new general manager, Jaime, has been putting in.

There are shared breakrooms and a conference room. The conference room can be reserved for business meetings in a very easy way. You are also able to use their forklift after you've completed training.

We had our hiccups in the beginning but since then, it's been a wonderful experience and I look forward to continuing my business with ReadySpaces! This facility is very clean indoors and out. The common lounge area is inviting and spacious. I appreciate having Wifi here too! It's pretty techy here where I get to use my phone to unlock the door and my own office is keypad entry. The facility manager, Andre is awesome. He's always quick to respond via email and in person. He's very warm and personable. And I appreciate that he's open to feedback for this location to help it be better for everyone.

My only qualm at this time is the women's restroom. It's always clean and well maintained, but I think it's a plumbing issue where the water comes back up and often doesn't flush. I understand this isn't something ReadySpaces can control.

content.body:
This is my first review ever on the Shopify marketplace after being a seller for 4 years. Never have I ever left a bad review just in general in my life as I know how hard it is to run a business and I am appreciative of other people's livelihoods. However this app needs to be completely removed from the Shopify App Store.
Do not download this app. This app, and their developer will try everything to overcharge you, they will receive the shipment, say that it was x10 heavier and send you a bill for thousands of dollars. I have contacted Shopify support to intervene in this dispute because it appears as this app is overcharging many other sellers (just read all of the reviews below mine). Will see what happens next but just believe me and stay away from these people. They use very sneaky elusive tactics to make sure they can get as much money as they can out of you.

classification.summary: ART Phenomena is urgently leaving Easyship due to alleged deceptive overcharging tactics on shipment weights, resulting in thousands of dollars in unexpected bills, and is actively warning others to avoid the service.

content.body: I'm happy so far. I used Easyship app for transition from crowdfunding to ecommerce.
